mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods yield negative 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4 percent month-over-month are followed by positive 20-day returns in greater than 70 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic output and corporate revenue growth, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal immediate supply chain contraction and falling business activity, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Two-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 65 percent of historical instances.
